mymods_error_not_a_mod: That folder is not a mod - no ebr-mod.json found in {folder}.
mymods_error_unreadable_detail: Could not read the mod in {folder}: {detail}.
setup_lead: This app has no accounts or servers of its own - your mods live in your own free GitHub account. This one-time setup connects the app to GitHub so it can back up and publish what you make.
setup_credentials_lead: The GitHub sign-in registered on this computer. Used for backing up and publishing your mods.
setup_switch_confirm: This makes your computer forget the saved GitHub sign-in for github.com - system-wide, not just in this app. Every tool that uses it will be affected: the next git push, pull, or fetch (in any terminal, editor, or Git client), as well as publishing from this app, will prompt you to sign in again.
setup_forks_lead: Your own copies of the mod projects on GitHub, where your mods are stored and published from.
setup_forks_unreachable: At least one saved fork could not be reached on GitHub. It may have been deleted or renamed - run setup again to recreate it.
setup_manual_intro: Create these forks in your browser, then run setup again:
setup_author_lead: Pre-filled when you create a new mod.
setup_clear_lead: Removes your saved fork locations and author details from this computer. Forgets your GitHub sign-in for this app, but does not affect your system-wide GitHub credentials for other tools.
setup_error_no_sign_in: Could not detect a GitHub sign-in. These tools push to GitHub over HTTPS, so an HTTPS sign-in is required. Set one up (Git Credential Manager will prompt you), then try again.
setup_error_switch_manual: Could not clear the saved GitHub credential automatically. Clear it yourself with `git credential reject`, then run setup again.
